---
title: "Get Started"
description: "Minimal end-to-end alignment example with in-memory data"
author: "Karel Dieussaert"
date: "2026-01-07"
categories: [Process]
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
jupyter: python3
---
# Get Started
This is the smallest complete `brdr` workflow:

1. Create an [Aligner](../reference/Aligner.qmd).
2. Load one thematic geometry and one reference geometry with [DictLoader](../reference/DictLoader.qmd).
3. Run `process(...)` for one `relevant_distance`.
4. Inspect the aligned result and the added/removed differences.

Use this example as the baseline before trying external loaders or advanced processors.

## Input and Output

- Input: two small polygons created from WKT.
- Output:
  - `result`: aligned geometry
  - `result_diff_plus`: area added by alignment
  - `result_diff_min`: area removed by alignment

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "basic example"
{{< include ../../examples/example_readme.py >}}
```
